zh.Tourcoing: 法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德圖爾可
zh.Gent: 比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugo II Zeger van Gent於1197年出生在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特時，他的父親Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele32歲，母親Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain17歲。他於1194年與Eudette de Champlitte結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1232年11月26日在他的家鄉享年35歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugo II Zeger van Gent于1197年出生在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫根特时，他的父亲Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele32岁，母亲Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain17岁。他于1194年与Eudette de Champlitte结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1232年11月26日在他的家乡享年35岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern) and French: nickname from Old French gent, Middle English gent(e) ‘high born, noble’, hence ‘noble of character or conduct, courteous’, also ‘nice, friendly’.
English: habitational name from Ghent (Gent in Dutch), a city in Flanders, Belgium. Compare Ghent .
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with gand ‘spell’, such as Gandolf.

variants.name: Jent
meanings: English: variant of   Gent  .

variants.name: Ghent
meanings: English: habitational name from Ghent (Gent in Dutch), a city in Flanders, Belgium. Compare Gent .

variants.name: Gentes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Henri, Lucien, Marcel, Normand.
French (Gentès): probably a nickname derived from Old French gent ‘high born, noble’, also ‘nice, friendly’.
German: patronymic from the personal name   Gent  .

variants.name: Genther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Otto.
German:
variant of   Gent  .

variants.name: Genthe
meanings: German: variant of Gente (see Gent ), or a habitational name from Gentha in Saxony-Anhalt, east of Wittenberg.

nameMeanings.name: Hugo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of   Hugh  , used throughout the Middle Ages in official documents, and now enjoying a revival as a given name.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

nameMeanings.name: Zeger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Luitger .

en.lifeSummary: When Hugo II Zeger van Gent was born in 1197, in Ghent, East Flanders, Belgium, his father, Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele, was 32 and his mother, Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain, was 17. He married Eudette de Champlitte in 1194. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 26 November 1232, in Gent, East Flanders, Belgium, at the age of 35.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugo II Zeger van Gent est né en 1197, à Gand, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, son père, Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Eudette de Champlitte en 1194. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 26 novembre 1232, à Gent, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, à l’âge de 35 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugo II Zeger van Gent 1197 in Gent, Ostflandern, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele 32 und seine Mutter Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain 17. Er hat 1194 Eudette de Champlitte geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 26. November 1232 im Alter von 35 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugo II Zeger van Gent è nato il nel 1197 nel Gent, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, il suo padre, Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Eudette de Champlitte nel 1194. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 26 novembre 1232, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 35 anni.

ja.Gent: ベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンジェント
ja.lifeSummary: Hugo II Zeger van Gentが1197年にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンジェントで生まれた時，父Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezeleは32歳，母Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdainは17歳であった。1194年にEudette de Champlitteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1232年11月26日に故郷で35歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugo II Zeger van Gent(이)가 1197년 벨기에 스트-블라아더 젠트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1194년 Eudette de Champlitte(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1232년 11월 26일 그의 고향에서 35세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugo II Zeger van Gent nasceu em 1197, em Gent, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, seu pai, Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Eudette de Champlitte em 1194. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 26 de novembro de 1232, em sua cidade natal, com 35 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugo II Zeger van Gent родился в 1197 г. в местности джент, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Eudette de Champlitte в 1194 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 26 ноября 1232 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 35 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugo II Zeger van Gent nació en 1197, en Gent, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, su papá, Zeger Steppo van Gent van Viggezele, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Beatrijs Hugo Anselm de Hosdain, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Eudette de Champlitte en 1194. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 26 de noviembre de 1232, en su pueblo, a la edad de 35 años.
